title: allium canadense subsp. lavendulare
intro: Allium canadense subsp. lavendulare, also known as Canadian wild garlic, is a subspecies of wild garlic native to North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Allium canadense subsp. lavendulare include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and boost the immune system, making it a natural remedy for colds and respiratory issues. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
